ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sustainability_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "agreement_level" TEXT /* Level of agreement with sustainability statements (e.g., Agreement, Neutral, Disagreement) */,
  "shop_less_often" INTEGER /* Percentage of consumers shopping less often */,
  "reduce_spending" INTEGER /* Percentage of consumers reducing their spending */,
  "changed_items" INTEGER /* Percentage of consumers changing the items they purchase */,
  "reduce_waste" INTEGER /* Percentage of consumers reducing waste */,
  "recycle_rate" INTEGER /* Percentage of consumers recycling */,
  "energy_consumption" INTEGER /* Average energy consumption of consumers */,
  "water_usage" INTEGER /* Average water usage of consumers */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "consumer_behavior" (
  "behavior_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each consumer behavior entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sustainability metric */,
  "consumer_segment" TEXT /* Segment of consumers exhibiting the behavior (e.g., Brand Loyal, Social Proof Seekers) */,
  "behavior_type" TEXT /* Type of behavior (e.g., one-time, recurring) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the behavior (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the behavior */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("behavior_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_consumer_behavior_consumer_segment FOREIGN KEY ("consumer_segment") REFERENCES consumer_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_consumer_behavior_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES sustainability_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "consumer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each consumer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the consumer segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the consumer segment */,
  "demographic_info" TEXT /* Demographic information of the segment (e.g., age, income, education) */,
  "psychographic_info" TEXT /* Psychographic information of the segment (e.g., values, lifestyle, interests) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "behavior_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the consumer behavior */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the survey was responded to */,
  "response_details" TEXT /* Detailed response from the consumer */,
  "response_score" INTEGER /* Score of the response (e.g., 1-5, where 5 is the highest) */,
  "survey_type" TEXT /* Type of survey (e.g., online, phone, in-person) */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Number of participants in the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_surveys_behavior_id FOREIGN KEY ("behavior_id") REFERENCES consumer_behavior ("behavior_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, marketing specialist, business leader) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., marketing, sales, product) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user last logged in */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "behavior_id" INTEGER /* ID of the consumer behavior data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., admin, read-only) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_behavior_id FOREIGN KEY ("behavior_id") REFERENCES consumer_behavior ("behavior_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction entry */,
  "behavior_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the consumer behavior being predicted */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value for the behavior */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction (e.g., 0-1) */,
  "model_used" TEXT /* Machine learning model used for prediction */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the prediction was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_behavior_id FOREIGN KEY ("behavior_id") REFERENCES consumer_behavior ("behavior_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retailer_info" (
  "retailer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retailer */,
  "retailer_name" TEXT /* Name of the retailer */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the retailer */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the retailer */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the retailer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retailer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "retailer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the retailer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_retailer_id FOREIGN KEY ("retailer_id") REFERENCES retailer_info ("retailer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sustainability_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product being scored */,
  "score" REAL /* Sustainability score of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the score (e.g., energy, waste, water) */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating of the score (e.g., A, B, C) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the score was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sustainability_scores_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)
